title: Selaginella pusilla
taxonomy: Selaginella pusilla is a species of plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. This family is part of the division Lycopodiophyta, which is a group of ancient vascular plants. Selaginella pusilla is classified as a member of the genus Selaginella, which consists of about 700 species. These plants are often referred to as resurrection plants due to their ability to survive in dry conditions. They are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world.

intro: Selaginella pusilla, also known as dwarf spike moss or spike moss, is a small, evergreen plant belonging to the family Selaginellaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the management of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Selaginella pusilla include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Selaginella pusilla is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly employed in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
